ops.text: fli (`uvx --from "flights[mcp]" fli flights HND OKA ...`) silently returns zero results for any search touching OKA: the Airport enum collapses OKA into an alias of NAH (both value "Naha Airport" — Tahuna, Indonesia), so the wire request searches HND->NAH (upstream punitarani/fli#131, ~44 duplicate groups affected). Workaround is library-level, not CLI: import fli.models.Airport, set `Airport["OKA"]._name_ = "OKA"` BEFORE importing fli.search (the encode path serializes member.name), and also patch `fli.search._decoders._AIRPORT_BY_CODE["OKA"]` (the decode cache iterates canonical members only, so response rows citing OKA raise AttributeError and every row gets skipped). Separate gotcha stacked on top: a "today" date computed in US-Pacific can already be over in JST — Google returns zero itineraries for a past-in-origin-timezone departure date, quietly, so same-day Japan hops priced after ~08:00 PDT need the next JST date.
